fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England, won by the West Indies, who defeated Australia in the final at Lord's.
India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2011 on home soil, defeating Sri Lanka in the final at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai. This was India's second World Cup title after 1983.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most runs in Test cricket (15,921) and the most international centuries (100) in history.
A standard One Day International (ODI) cricket match consists of 50 overs per side, while a Twenty20 (T20) match consists of 20 overs per side.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
A standard football (soccer) match consists of two 45-minute halves, for a total of 90 minutes of regular play, plus stoppage time.
Lionel Messi won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in 2022, defeating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw in Qatar.
The offside rule states an attacking player is offside if they are nearer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-last opponent when the ball is played to them.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
In badminton, a standard match is played as the best of three games, with each game played to 21 points (win by 2, capped at 30).
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics, and later won bronze at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics.
The Uber Cup is the women's equivalent of the Thomas Cup, contested as the premier international women's team badminton championship since 1956.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, the French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in the Open Era, surpassing Rafael Nadal and Roger Federer.
Wimbledon, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam still played on grass courts.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most of any player in the Open Era.
The NBA was founded in 1946 and is the premier professional basketball league in the United States and Canada.
A standard NBA basketball game consists of four 12-minute quarters, totaling 48 minutes of regulation play.
Kareem Abdul-Jabbar was the NBA's all-time leading scorer for nearly four decades before being surpassed by LeBron James in 2023.
The United States men's basketball 'Dream Team' at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics is widely regarded as the greatest sports team ever assembled, featuring Michael Jordan, Magic Johnson, and Larry Bird.
